betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Fuel Reprocessor§f:
The Fuel Reprocessor (nuclearcraft:fuel_reprocessor) is a single-block machine that recovers usable actinides and fission products from spent nuclear fuel, allowing breeders and reactors to recycle depleted fuel back into useful feedstock.

§6Slot Layout (FuelReprocessorRecipes.java:10)§f:
1 item input → 0 fluid input → 8 item outputs → 0 fluid output. Each cycle produces up to eight item outputs (four main products, two byproducts, and two waste streams).

§6Crafting Recipe (CraftingRecipeHandler.java:76)§f:
Pattern "PBP / TCT / PAP" in a 3×3 grid. Letters: P = plateBasic, B = ingotBoron, T = ingotTough, A = actuator, C = chassis. Yields 1 Fuel Reprocessor.

§6What It Does (FuelReprocessorRecipes.java:13-60)§f:
addReprocessingRecipes() is invoked for each of NC's standard fuel families (TBU, LEU233, HEU233, LEU235, HEU235, LEN236, HEN236, LEP239, HEP239, LEP241, HEP241, MIX239, MIX241, LEA242, HEA242, LECm243, HECm243, LECm245, HECm245, LECm247, HECm247, LEB248, HEB248, LECf249, HECf249, LECf251, HECf251). For each fuel family, four parallel recipes are added: one each for TRISO, Oxide, Nitride, and Zirconium-Alloy (ZA) fuel forms.

§6Recipe Structure (FuelReprocessorRecipes.java:62-68)§f:
Input is always 9 ingots of `ingotDepleted{fuel}{Form}`. Outputs are 4 main actinide carbide/oxide/nitride/ZA products, 2 radioactive wastes (one guaranteed 50% chance dust, one lower chance), and 2 bonus outputs (dustGraphite for TRISO, dustZirconium for ZA, empty for Oxide/Nitride). The `m × r` factor scales waste output by enrichment (LE=0.5, HE=1.5) × reprocessing rate (50 or 60 RF/operation).

§6Fission Product Recovery§f:
Standard outputs include Uranium-238, Plutonium-239/241/242, Neptunium-236/237, Americium-241/242/243, Curium-243/245/246/247, Berkelium-247/248, Californium-249/250/251/252. The fissile isotopes (U-235, Pu-239, Np-237, Am-242m, Cm-245, Cm-247, Bk-248, Cf-249, Cf-251) are recovered as carbides/oxides/nitrides/ZAs for re-enrichment or re-fabrication.

§6Radioactive Waste Streams (FuelReprocessorRecipes.java:64)§f:
The two waste slots are filled with `dust{waste1}` (50% chance scaled by enrichment × reprocessing rate) and `dust{waste2}` (the inverse chance). Waste types cycle by fuel family: Strontium-90 + Cesium-137 for uranium fuels, Strontium-90 + Promethium-147 for plutonium/americium fuels, Molybdenum + Promethium-147 for curium fuels, Ruthenium-106 + Europium-155 for californium fuels.

§6IC2 Compatibility (FuelReprocessorRecipes.java:58-59)§f:
Two additional recipes handle IC2 depleted fuel cells: 1 depleted IC2 U fuel cell → 2 nearly-enriched U cells + 1 re-enriched U cell + 1 MOX cell; 1 depleted IC2 MOX fuel cell → 4 MOX cells.

§eTip§f: Reprocessing is the heart of any self-sustaining nuclear economy. Build a Fuel Reprocessor line early — every cycle recovers isotopes that would otherwise need fresh mining, and the radioactive waste dusts are useful for RTGs and shield plating.

§eTip§f: Pair the Fuel Reprocessor with a Decay Hastener and Isotope Separator to turn waste dusts back into useful isotopes, or send them straight to a Melter to convert into fluid form for further processing.

§cWarning§f: Radioactive waste dusts emit radiation. Keep the Fuel Reprocessor in a shielded room or use a Rad-Shielded Container, and never carry the output dust stacks in your inventory without proper PPE.
